pi_items.verified_value: Descriptively accurate but not the official Natural Earth name; dataset is 'Admin 0 - Countries' published initially as 'Natural Earth Countries'
pi_items.source: Natural Earth documentation (naturalearthdata.com) and setup_task.sh showing initial publication as 'Natural Earth Countries'
Natural Earth official documentation and GitHub repository confirm 110m scale

pi_items.verified_value: The dataset contains 258 countries including both sovereign states AND dependent territories, not just 209 sovereign states
pi_items.source: Natural Earth documentation states: 'There are 258 countries... Countries distinguish between metropolitan (homeland) and independent and semi-independent portions of sovereign states' and 'Dependent territories included'
Cartographic standards define scales with denominators above 1:500,000 as small-scale; 1:110 million is very small-scale suitable for world/continental mapping
Dataset contains 258 countries; officially named 'Admin 0 - Countries'
Dataset represents political/administrative boundaries; part of Natural Earth Cultural Vectors collection
Data consists of administrative boundary polygons for country borders
Official dataset name is 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries'; 'Admin 0' denotes country-level administrative boundaries
Natural Earth uses WGS 84 as standard SRS; EPSG:4326 is the standard code for WGS 84; setup_task.sh sets '-a_srs EPSG:4326'
GeoServer WMS best practice for published layers; setup_task.sh tracks this as a configuration parameter

privileged_info_summary: The layer uses real public data from Natural Earth's 110m Admin 0 Countries dataset, which contains 258 countries (including both sovereign states and dependent territories) at 1:110 million scale, the standard scale for small-scale thematic world mapping. The data uses EPSG:4326 (WGS 84) coordinate system, which is the standard for GPS and global geographic data. The expected title 'World Political Boundaries (1:110m)' accurately describes the data's purpose, and most metadata keywords (countries, political, boundaries, natural-earth, admin-0) are verified as appropriate. The abstract phrase 'sovereign states' is technically incomplete since the dataset includes dependent territories, making 'sovereign states and dependent territories' more accurate.
